title: Building Power Headroom
learningObjectives: Keep total power below component limits
Adjust voltage and resistance to increase headroom
Interpret power as V × I in a series loop
introText: Headroom keeps parts safe. You'll raise resistance and trim voltage until total power drops comfortably under a limit.

steps.instructionText: Add a 15V source and a resistor, wired into a closed series loop.
steps.feedbackOnSuccess: Loop is closed. Time to check power.
steps.feedbackOnFailure: Connect both terminals of the source and resistor to close the loop.

steps.instructionText: Increase the resistor until total power falls below 0.4 W.
steps.feedbackOnSuccess: Power is under 0.4 W — safe for most small resistors.
steps.feedbackOnFailure: Raise resistance or lower voltage until power drops under 0.4 W.

steps.instructionText: Reduce the source voltage slightly to bring power under 0.25 W for extra margin.
steps.feedbackOnSuccess: Great — power is below 0.25 W, giving comfortable headroom.
steps.feedbackOnFailure: Dial down the source or raise resistance further to drop below 0.25 W.

options: To reduce stress and heat on components
questions.explanation: Lowering power keeps components cooler and extends their life.

questions.questionText: How can you quickly reduce power in a series circuit?
questions.explanation: Power = V × I; lowering V or lowering I (by raising R) reduces total power.
